title: Source-backed overlap claim for NIST AI RMF and governance boundary
summary: Proposes that NIST AI RMF governance and AI risk-management language should be recorded as overlapping with, not equivalent to, the Admissibility Wiki term Governance Boundary.

claim_comparison.stegverse_term: Governance Boundary distinguishes which layer may authorize, publish, explain, evaluate, or prove a transition claim.
claim_comparison.external_term: NIST AI RMF addresses AI risk management for organizations designing, developing, deploying, or using AI systems.

claim_comparison.stegverse_term: Governance Boundary is concerned with standing at proposal, review, publication, and commit time.
claim_comparison.external_term: NIST AI RMF is lifecycle-oriented risk-management guidance, but it does not define StegVerse commit-time standing.

claim_comparison.stegverse_term: Governance Boundary prevents public pages, proposals, and receipts from overclaiming execution or proof authority.
claim_comparison.external_term: NIST AI RMF frames voluntary risk management and trustworthiness considerations rather than StegVerse-specific execution authority.

claim_comparison.stegverse_term: Governance Boundary requires source-of-truth and authority-role clarity before standing is claimed.
claim_comparison.external_term: NIST AI RMF provides risk management guidance but does not serve as a decision receipt for a specific StegVerse transition.

claimed_relationships.notes: Both terms address structured governance of AI systems and responsible risk management. The relationship is not equivalent because Governance Boundary specifically separates publication, vocabulary, continuity, proof, and commit-time admissibility authority.
Trustworthiness considerations are adjacent because they inform responsible AI design and use, but do not by themselves define receipt-bound standing or commit-time admissibility.

mismatches: NIST AI RMF does not encode Site/Wiki/Continuity/StegCore role separation.
NIST AI RMF does not mint a StegVerse decision receipt for a specific transition.
NIST AI RMF does not by itself determine commit-time admissibility or consequence standing.
risk_of_overclaiming: Calling NIST AI RMF governance language equivalent to Governance Boundary could imply NIST endorsement of StegVerse terminology.
Calling the terms equivalent could imply that voluntary AI risk-management guidance is itself a StegVerse execution-authority boundary.
Calling the terms equivalent could erase the distinction between broad AI risk management and commit-time transition standing.
proposed_text: NIST AI RMF governance and AI risk-management language overlaps with Governance Boundary where both address structured governance of AI systems and responsible management of AI-related risk. It is not recorded as equivalent because Governance Boundary specifically separates publication, vocabulary, continuity, proof, and commit-time admissibility authority within the StegVerse transition-governance model.
non_claims: This proposal does not assert that NIST AI RMF is equivalent to Governance Boundary.
This proposal does not claim NIST endorsement of StegVerse terminology.
This proposal does not update the glossary by itself.
This proposal does not create admissibility standing.
This proposal requires a decision record before acceptance.
